revision.comment: Honda Chikaatsu text
revision.text:
'''''Reigakushō''''' (霊学抄), or "A Summary of Spiritology," outlines the core tenets of the spiritual system developed by Honda Chikaatsu. It is a foundational document that explains his unique interpretation of ancient Shinto (''Ko-Shinto'') practices.
:'''A Summary of Spiritology''' :''(Reigaku Shō)''
:The Spiritology (''Reigaku'') inherent to the Imperial Land consists of the three pillars of Soul Pacification (''Chinkon''), Returning to the Divine (''Kishin''), and Grand Divination (''Futomani''). Magical rites (''Kin'en'') are but one function of Soul Pacification.
:'''Grand Divination (太占, ''Futomani'')'''
:When the ''Kojiki'' states that the gods said to Izanagi and Izanami, "divine by Grand Divination" (''futomani ni uraete''), this refers to the formless confronting the formless—something beyond human thought or deliberation.
:The passage in the chapter on the Heavenly Rock Cave, which says, "the shoulder-bone of a true stag from Mount Ame-no-Kaguyama was extracted," refers to a formed type of Grand Divination. The selection of the fields for the ''Daijōsai'' (the first ceremonial offering of rice by a newly enthroned emperor) is, even today, determined by Grand Divination.
:'''The Three Great Methods of Grand Divination'''
:1. '''Method of Forms (形象法, ''Keishō-hō''):''' To make a judgment by observing the forms of heaven, earth, wind, clouds, and all other things. : '' Scapulimancy (divination by deer bones), plastromancy (divination by turtle shells), and other forms such as foot-reading, rice-reading, and righteousness-divination, though their methods differ, all belong to the Method of Forms.
:2. '''Method of Sounds (声音法, ''Seion-hō''):''' To make a judgment by listening to the sounds of thunder, wind, and all other things. : '' Crossroads-divination, song-divination, divination by the sound of a person's voice, and the like all belong to the Method of Sounds.
:3. '''Method of Numerology (算数法, ''Sansū-hō''):''' To make a judgment according to the mathematical principles of the celestial numbers from one to ten, arranged as warp and weft. : '' All methods of this kind belong to the Method of Numerology.
:The above are the methods of the manifest world. In the divine realm, the gods observe the formless, listen to the soundless, and calculate the numberless. Therefore, you should know that their methods are vastly different from the methods of the manifest world.
:'''Rules of Study (学則, ''Gakusoku'')'''
:The silent revelations of God are made through the three great principles of Spirit, Body, and Power, which we observe by looking up and down at the universe.
:1. One must, by observing the true forms of heaven and earth, contemplate the Body of the True God. :2. One must, by observing the flawless workings and transformations of all existence, contemplate the Power of the True God. :3. One must, by comprehending the heart and nature of living things, contemplate the Spirit-Soul of the True God.
:With this living scripture, one can know the reason why the True God is the True God. What use is there in studying man-made books and scrolls? There is only the unchanging and immutable true mirror and true principle.
:* '''Body (体, ''Tai'')''' refers to physical substance; it is what chemistry calls the elements. :* '''Power (力, ''Ryoku'')''' refers to the force of motion; the Earth's revolution around the great Sun creating the four seasons, and the Earth's own rotation creating day and night—these are all the workings of Power. :* '''Spirit (霊, ''Rei'')''' refers to the divine. Our own spiritual souls also belong to this.
:'''Soul Pacification (鎮魂, ''Chinkon'')'''
:The method of Soul Pacification is the great foundation of Spiritology, so it is imperative that we determine its origins and clarify its derivative methods. Therefore, I will now state its origins based on the Imperial Canons.
:The deity Izanagi-no-Mikoto declared, "Amaterasu-Ōmikami shall rule the High Plain of Heaven," and taking the string of beads from his divine neck and shaking them so they chimed, he bestowed them upon Amaterasu-Ōmikami. Therefore, this jewel is venerated and called the God of the August Storehouse Shelf (''Mikuratana-no-kami''). This was a divine decree meant to have her spirit-soul attach to it and make her the sovereign of the manifest heavens. And this jewel was then handed down from Amaterasu-Ōmikami to the Imperial Grandson, Ninigi-no-Mikoto. The facts of that event appear in the ''Kojiki'' as, "the beads of joy, the mirror of joy, keep them close." Since the Three Sacred Treasures were bestowed as the symbol of ruling the imperial throne, it goes without saying that the emperors of every generation—and all the myriad people who took the emperor's heart as their own—have revered and worshipped them without fail. For this reason, based on the very principle of why a god is a god, they have received all manner of benefits and spiritual efficacies. That this is recorded in our national histories, shining brightly and vying in radiance with the sun and moon, you should know is no falsehood of mine. As for our current teachings, there is not a single further passage to cite. Therefore, I will only transmit the procedure for this soul-summoning.
:This method of Soul Pacification is a divinely bestowed sacred law and a rule of study for both the manifest and divine worlds. It extends from the Emperor's work of governing the nation and pacifying the realm above, down to the people's foundation for cultivating the self and ordering the household below. It is furthermore the basis for perceiving the formless divine realm. Therefore, you should secretly carry this in your heart morning and evening, and in your leisure from work, you should reverently reflect upon it and practice it, learning the movements and activities of your spiritual soul. More detailed matters will be lectured on at a later date, where I will use citations to reveal its foundations and feel, or perhaps demonstrate, its spiritual wonders, so that we may increasingly establish, on behalf of heaven, a great and meritorious power for ten thousand generations. This is our duty as the head of all creation.
:The ''Ryō no Gige'' [an ancient commentary on law] states:
:鎮 (''Chin'') means to soothe (''an''). The yang energy of a person is called the soul (''kon''). This speaks of summoning the wandering vital soul (''un-kon'') and keeping it in the central office (''chū-fu'') of the body.
:Therefore, it is called Soul Pacification (''Chinkon'').
:'''Returning to the Divine (帰神, ''Kishin'')'''
:The method of Returning to the Divine is also called the method of Esoteric Rites (''Yūsai'').
:The way of achieving communion with the divine realm is most supreme and most precious; it is not something to be spoken of lightly. Although our nation's ancient classics frequently record its actual practice, the way of ritual decayed in the middle ages, and the art has now been lost for a long time. By relying on divine law, we can restore the ancient way. This is the ultimate of profound principles, the great foundation for ruling the nation that the Imperial Ancestors transmitted to the Imperial Grandson, and the esoteric heart of ritual. The method of Esoteric Rites is most strict and most serious; there are things that must not be practiced if one is not the right person. The intention not to transmit it carelessly lies here. Nevertheless, if your spirit does not bend before ten thousand difficulties and you strive for it yourself without stopping, you will eventually be able to reach its wondrous state. Let those who receive this transmission in the future understand this well. The way of communicating with the unseen world lies solely in dedicated practice. Here, I will show the method.
:1. One must cleanse one's body and clothes. :2. One must select a secluded place and a quiet house. :3. One must compose one's body, close one's eyes, and sit silently. :4. One must remove all delusive thoughts. :5. One must exhaust all sensory perception and extinguish all conceptual thought. :6. One must strive to keep one's heart-mind clear and pure, not to be disturbed by sensations. :7. One must, with single-minded focus, silently contemplate one's own spirit-soul reaching the presence of the great god Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Ōkami.
:The above seven articles clarify the essentials of self-cultivation.
:Through Esoteric Rites, one communes with the sovereign of the universe and comes into direct contact with the eight million gods. When one becomes capable of this practice, it is so great there is nothing outside it, and so small there is nothing inside it. There is no far or near, no large or small, no wide or narrow, no light or dark; from the past, to the present, to the future, there is nothing with which one cannot communicate. This is the wondrous method of acting as the gods do (''kannagara'').
:There are things that one should always cherish. Here I will list their outlines.
:1. The spirit-soul is a bestowal from the divine realm and is thus a divided spirit (''bunrei''). Therefore, respect it yourself and do not be deceived by phantoms, demons, and the like. :2. One must clearly distinguish between right and wrong, principle and non-principle. :3. One must always recite the divine canons and keep the virtues of the gods in memory. :4. One must fully comprehend that in the unseen world, there exists a realm of true gods and a realm of demonic phantoms. :5. The true gods have one hundred and eighty-one ranks; the demonic phantoms have the same. :6. One must know that the realm of true gods and the realm of false gods have a distinction between right and wrong and a difference in status, and that their variance is as great as that between heaven and the abyss. :7. If one's spirit is correct, one will commune with the true gods; if it is corrupt, one will commune with the false gods. The correctness, corruption, wisdom, or foolishness of one's spirit corresponds directly to the unseen world. One must be extremely cautious.
:Although the above lists the outlines, the affairs of the unseen world are profound, spiritual, and wondrous, and there are aspects of their ultimate state that cannot be put into words. It depends solely on the practitioner's cultivation.
:The method of Returning to the Divine is most precious and most serious. For those who practice it, I will now show the essentials that must be known.
:'''Table of Methods for Returning to the Divine'''
:'''Formless (無形, ''Mukei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:'''Formed (有形, ''Yūkei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:* '''Total of Formed and Formless: 18 methods.'''
:* '''Realm of Demonic Phantoms (妖魅界, ''Yōmi-kai''):''' The same 18 methods as above. : '' The practices of shrine maidens (''miko'') or Hokke (Lotus Sutra) monks are of a lower-than-the-lowest-of-the-low grade, outside of these. : '' What is called the Realm of False Gods (''Jashin-kai'') is the same as the Realm of Demonic Phantoms. : '' These can be further divided into 362 methods.
:What is critical in Returning to the Divine is the Spirit Examiner (''Saniwa''). If one is not the right person for this role, it cannot be done. It requires one whose attention is thorough, who has courage, who possesses knowledge, and who is swift in clarifying principle and non-principle.
:1. One must inquire into the past, present, and future. :2. One must not proceed without discerning whether the spirit is a true god or a false god. :3. One must not proceed without knowing the upper, middle, and lower ranks of the gods. :4. One must not proceed without knowing the great deeds of the god. :5. One must not proceed without knowing the Wild Soul, Harmonious Soul, Fortunate Soul, and Wondrous Soul. :6. One must not be without the ability to distinguish between Heavenly Gods and Earthly Deities. :7. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have three grades. :8. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have public and private possessions/channels.
==Source== *[https://ja.wikisource.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E5%AD%A6%E6%8A%84 霊学抄] on Wikisource
[[Category:Honda Chikaatsu]]

revision.comment: Japanese text
revision.text:
'''''Reigakushō''''' (霊学抄), or "A Summary of Spiritology," outlines the core tenets of the spiritual system developed by Honda Chikaatsu. It is a foundational document that explains his unique interpretation of ancient Shinto (''Ko-Shinto'') practices.
==Original text== ;霊学抄
皇国固有の霊学は鎮魂、帰神、太占の三にして、禁厭は鎮魂の作用の一なり。
=== 太占 === *古事記に伊邪那岐、伊邪那美に神が、「太占に卜へて」（布斗麻通にうらへて）とあるは無形を以て無形に対するもの、人間の思義す可らざるものなり。
*天岩戸の条に「天香山の真男鹿の肩を拔きて」とあるは、有形の太占なり。大甞祭のゆき、すきの御田を定むるは今日にても太占に依る。
=== 太占乃三大法 === *'''形象法'''。天地風雲其他一切萬の形を観じて判決す。 :鹿卜、亀卜、其他足占米占義占、法異なりと雖も皆之形象法の属とす。
*'''声音法'''。雷風其他万物の声音に聴て判決す。 :辻占、謡占、語声等一切声音法に属す。
*'''算数法'''。天数一より十に至り経緯の数理に依て判決す。故に是の如き法悉く皆算数法に属す。 :以上は現世の法にして神界にては無形に観じ無声に聴き無算に算へ給ふ。故に現世の法とは格別なるら知るべし。
=== 学則 === 神の黙示は則ち吾俯仰観察する宇宙の霊体力の三大を以てす。
*一、天地の真象を観察して真神の体を思考す可し。 *一、万有の運化の毫差無きを以て真神の力を思考す可し。 *一、活物の心性を覚悟して真神の霊魂を思考す可し。
以上の活経典有り真神の真神たる故由を知る。何ぞ人為の書巻を学習するを用ゐん哉。唯不変不易の真鑑実理ある而己。
'''体'''とは則ち物体にして化学の所謂元素之なり。
'''力'''とは則ち運動の力にして、地球の大陽を一周して春夏秋冬をなし、地球の自転して昼夜ら為す等皆力の処為なり。
'''霊'''とは則ち神也。吾人の霊魂亦之に属す。
=== 鎮魂 === 鎮魂の法は霊学の大本なれば、其の原由を論定し其の末法を講明せざる可らず。故に今皇典に拠て其由来を述ぶ。
伊邪那岐命曰く、天照大御神は高天原を知食すべしと詔玉ひて、御首玉を母由良に取り由良加して天照大御神に賜ひき。故に此玉を斎き奉りて御倉棚神と云ふ。是其霊魂を附着して現天の主宰たらしめん事を神定め賜ひしものなり。而して此玉ら天照大神より皇孫二二岐命へ御授けあり。其時の事実の古事記に見えて、男喜志玉男喜志鏡斂とあり。三種の神宝を帝位知食す御印として下し賜ひしょり以来、御代御代の帝王は申すも更なり、其御心を心として万民悉く尊奉崇敬して怠らざりし故に、神の神たる所以の理由よりして、万般の利益霊験ら蒙りし事国史に昭々として日月と共に光を争ふと云ふも我誣言に非るら知るべし。而して今本教に於るや更に一章を引証すべきなし。故に唯其招魂の作法を伝授せんとする也。
此鎮魂の法は天授の神法にして現世神界の学則なれば、上は天皇の治国平天下の御事よりして、下は人民修身斎家の基本、続て無形の神界を探知するの基礎なれば、宜敷朝夕之を懐中に秘し、事業の閑暇は謹て是を省み之を行ひ、霊魂の運転活動を学習すべし。尚詳細の事は後日講じ、引証を以て其基く処を現し、其霊妙なる処を感じ或は示して、益々天に代る大功力を千万世に建んとす。是則ち万物に長たるの義務なり。
:令義解に曰く
鎮者安也。人之陽気曰魂。言招離遊之運魂。留身体中府。
故謂之鎮魂。
=== 帰神 === 帰神の法を幽斎の法といふ。
神界に感合するの道は至尊至貴、濫に語る可き者に非ず。吾朝古典往々其実績を載すと雖も、中世祭祀の道衰へ其術を失ふ既に久し。神法に依り其古に復す。是即ち玄理の窮極、皇祖の以て皇孫に伝へし治国の大本にして祭祀の蘊奥なり。蓋し幽斎の法たる至厳至重、深く戒慎し其人に非れば行ふ可らざる者あり。濫に伝ふ可らざる意玆に存す。然りと雖も其精神萬難に撓まず。自ら彊めて止まされば竟に能く其妙境に達する事を得ん。後の此伝を受くる者厥れ之を諒せよ。幽冥に通ずるの道唯其れ專修に在り。玆に其法を示す。
*一、身体衣服を清潔にす可し。 *二、幽邃の地閑静の家を撰む可し。 *三、身体を整へ瞑目静座す可し。 *四、一切の妄想を除去す可し。 *五、感覚を蕩尽し意念を断滅す可し。 *六、心神を清澄にして感触の為に擾れざるを務む可し。 *七、一意に吾霊魂の天御中主大神の御許に至る事を黙念すべし。
右七章は自修の要を明らかにす。
幽斎は宇宙の主宰に感合し親しく八百万神に接す。其の修し得るに至ては至大無外、至小無内、無遠近、無大小、無広狭、無明暗、過去と現在と未来とを問はず一も通ぜざるは無し。是即ち惟神の妙法。
常に服膺すべき者あり。玆に其概を挙ぐ。
*一、霊魂は神界の賦与にして即ち分霊なれば、自ら之を尊重し妖魅等の為に誑かさる、事勿れ。 *二、正邪理非の分別を明かにす可し。 *三、常に神典を誦読し神徳を記憶す可し。 *四、幽冥に正神界と妖魅界と在る事を了得す可し。 *五、正神に百八十一の階級あり、妖魅亦之に同じ。 *六、正神界と邪神界とは正邪の別尊卑の差あり、其異る亦天淵の違あるを知る可し。 *七、精神正しければ即ち正神に感合し、邪なれば即ち邪神に感合す。精神の正邪賢愚は直に幽冥に応ず。最も戒慎すべし。
以上はその概を掲ぐと雖も、幽冥の事たる深遠霊妙、其至処は之を言ふ能はざる者あり。唯其人の修業に存す。
帰神の法たる至貴至重なり。行ふ者須く知る可きの要を玆に示す。
=== 帰神標目 === {| class="wikitable" |+ 無形 | 自感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 他感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 神感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |}
:合九法
{| class="wikitable" |+ 有形 | 自感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 他感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 神感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |}
:合九法
有形無形合拾八法。
妖魅界 前と同じく拾八法。
:巫或は法華僧の行は此等外下々下等也。
:邪神界と称するも妖魅界に同じ。
:之を分て三百六拾二法とす。
帰神に重要なるは審神者とす。其人に非れば即ち能はざる者也。其注意周到にして胆力あり、学識ありて理非を明にするに速かなるを要す。
*一、過去現在未来を伺ふ可し。 *二、真神なるや偽神なるや弁ぜずば在る可らず。 *三、神の上中下の品位を知らずば有る可らず。 *四、神の功業を知らずば有る可らず。 *五、荒魂和魂幸魂奇魂を知らずば有る可らず。 *六、天神地祇の分別無かる可らず。 *七、神に三等有るを知らずば有る可らず。 *八、神に公憑私憑あるを知らずば有る可らず。
==English translation== :'''A Summary of Spiritology''' :''(Reigaku Shō)''
:The Spiritology (''Reigaku'') inherent to the Imperial Land consists of the three pillars of Soul Pacification (''Chinkon''), Returning to the Divine (''Kishin''), and Grand Divination (''Futomani''). Magical rites (''Kin'en'') are but one function of Soul Pacification.
:'''Grand Divination (太占, ''Futomani'')'''
:When the ''Kojiki'' states that the gods said to Izanagi and Izanami, "divine by Grand Divination" (''futomani ni uraete''), this refers to the formless confronting the formless—something beyond human thought or deliberation.
:The passage in the chapter on the Heavenly Rock Cave, which says, "the shoulder-bone of a true stag from Mount Ame-no-Kaguyama was extracted," refers to a formed type of Grand Divination. The selection of the fields for the ''Daijōsai'' (the first ceremonial offering of rice by a newly enthroned emperor) is, even today, determined by Grand Divination.
:'''The Three Great Methods of Grand Divination'''
:1. '''Method of Forms (形象法, ''Keishō-hō''):''' To make a judgment by observing the forms of heaven, earth, wind, clouds, and all other things. : '' Scapulimancy (divination by deer bones), plastromancy (divination by turtle shells), and other forms such as foot-reading, rice-reading, and righteousness-divination, though their methods differ, all belong to the Method of Forms.
:2. '''Method of Sounds (声音法, ''Seion-hō''):''' To make a judgment by listening to the sounds of thunder, wind, and all other things. : '' Crossroads-divination, song-divination, divination by the sound of a person's voice, and the like all belong to the Method of Sounds.
:3. '''Method of Numerology (算数法, ''Sansū-hō''):''' To make a judgment according to the mathematical principles of the celestial numbers from one to ten, arranged as warp and weft. : '' All methods of this kind belong to the Method of Numerology.
:The above are the methods of the manifest world. In the divine realm, the gods observe the formless, listen to the soundless, and calculate the numberless. Therefore, you should know that their methods are vastly different from the methods of the manifest world.

:The silent revelations of God are made through the three great principles of Spirit, Body, and Power, which we observe by looking up and down at the universe.
:1. One must, by observing the true forms of heaven and earth, contemplate the Body of the True God. :2. One must, by observing the flawless workings and transformations of all existence, contemplate the Power of the True God. :3. One must, by comprehending the heart and nature of living things, contemplate the Spirit-Soul of the True God.
:With this living scripture, one can know the reason why the True God is the True God. What use is there in studying man-made books and scrolls? There is only the unchanging and immutable true mirror and true principle.
:* '''Body (体, ''Tai'')''' refers to physical substance; it is what chemistry calls the elements. :* '''Power (力, ''Ryoku'')''' refers to the force of motion; the Earth's revolution around the great Sun creating the four seasons, and the Earth's own rotation creating day and night—these are all the workings of Power. :* '''Spirit (霊, ''Rei'')''' refers to the divine. Our own spiritual souls also belong to this.
:'''Soul Pacification (鎮魂, ''Chinkon'')'''
:The method of Soul Pacification is the great foundation of Spiritology, so it is imperative that we determine its origins and clarify its derivative methods. Therefore, I will now state its origins based on the Imperial Canons.
:The deity Izanagi-no-Mikoto declared, "Amaterasu-Ōmikami shall rule the High Plain of Heaven," and taking the string of beads from his divine neck and shaking them so they chimed, he bestowed them upon Amaterasu-Ōmikami. Therefore, this jewel is venerated and called the God of the August Storehouse Shelf (''Mikuratana-no-kami''). This was a divine decree meant to have her spirit-soul attach to it and make her the sovereign of the manifest heavens. And this jewel was then handed down from Amaterasu-Ōmikami to the Imperial Grandson, Ninigi-no-Mikoto. The facts of that event appear in the ''Kojiki'' as, "the beads of joy, the mirror of joy, keep them close." Since the Three Sacred Treasures were bestowed as the symbol of ruling the imperial throne, it goes without saying that the emperors of every generation—and all the myriad people who took the emperor's heart as their own—have revered and worshipped them without fail. For this reason, based on the very principle of why a god is a god, they have received all manner of benefits and spiritual efficacies. That this is recorded in our national histories, shining brightly and vying in radiance with the sun and moon, you should know is no falsehood of mine. As for our current teachings, there is not a single further passage to cite. Therefore, I will only transmit the procedure for this soul-summoning.
:This method of Soul Pacification is a divinely bestowed sacred law and a rule of study for both the manifest and divine worlds. It extends from the Emperor's work of governing the nation and pacifying the realm above, down to the people's foundation for cultivating the self and ordering the household below. It is furthermore the basis for perceiving the formless divine realm. Therefore, you should secretly carry this in your heart morning and evening, and in your leisure from work, you should reverently reflect upon it and practice it, learning the movements and activities of your spiritual soul. More detailed matters will be lectured on at a later date, where I will use citations to reveal its foundations and feel, or perhaps demonstrate, its spiritual wonders, so that we may increasingly establish, on behalf of heaven, a great and meritorious power for ten thousand generations. This is our duty as the head of all creation.
:The ''Ryō no Gige'' [an ancient commentary on law] states:
:鎮 (''Chin'') means to soothe (''an''). The yang energy of a person is called the soul (''kon''). This speaks of summoning the wandering vital soul (''un-kon'') and keeping it in the central office (''chū-fu'') of the body.
:Therefore, it is called Soul Pacification (''Chinkon'').
:'''Returning to the Divine (帰神, ''Kishin'')'''
:The method of Returning to the Divine is also called the method of Esoteric Rites (''Yūsai'').
:The way of achieving communion with the divine realm is most supreme and most precious; it is not something to be spoken of lightly. Although our nation's ancient classics frequently record its actual practice, the way of ritual decayed in the middle ages, and the art has now been lost for a long time. By relying on divine law, we can restore the ancient way. This is the ultimate of profound principles, the great foundation for ruling the nation that the Imperial Ancestors transmitted to the Imperial Grandson, and the esoteric heart of ritual. The method of Esoteric Rites is most strict and most serious; there are things that must not be practiced if one is not the right person. The intention not to transmit it carelessly lies here. Nevertheless, if your spirit does not bend before ten thousand difficulties and you strive for it yourself without stopping, you will eventually be able to reach its wondrous state. Let those who receive this transmission in the future understand this well. The way of communicating with the unseen world lies solely in dedicated practice. Here, I will show the method.
:1. One must cleanse one's body and clothes. :2. One must select a secluded place and a quiet house. :3. One must compose one's body, close one's eyes, and sit silently. :4. One must remove all delusive thoughts. :5. One must exhaust all sensory perception and extinguish all conceptual thought. :6. One must strive to keep one's heart-mind clear and pure, not to be disturbed by sensations. :7. One must, with single-minded focus, silently contemplate one's own spirit-soul reaching the presence of the great god Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Ōkami.
:The above seven articles clarify the essentials of self-cultivation.
:Through Esoteric Rites, one communes with the sovereign of the universe and comes into direct contact with the eight million gods. When one becomes capable of this practice, it is so great there is nothing outside it, and so small there is nothing inside it. There is no far or near, no large or small, no wide or narrow, no light or dark; from the past, to the present, to the future, there is nothing with which one cannot communicate. This is the wondrous method of acting as the gods do (''kannagara'').
:There are things that one should always cherish. Here I will list their outlines.
:1. The spirit-soul is a bestowal from the divine realm and is thus a divided spirit (''bunrei''). Therefore, respect it yourself and do not be deceived by phantoms, demons, and the like. :2. One must clearly distinguish between right and wrong, principle and non-principle. :3. One must always recite the divine canons and keep the virtues of the gods in memory. :4. One must fully comprehend that in the unseen world, there exists a realm of true gods and a realm of demonic phantoms. :5. The true gods have one hundred and eighty-one ranks; the demonic phantoms have the same. :6. One must know that the realm of true gods and the realm of false gods have a distinction between right and wrong and a difference in status, and that their variance is as great as that between heaven and the abyss. :7. If one's spirit is correct, one will commune with the true gods; if it is corrupt, one will commune with the false gods. The correctness, corruption, wisdom, or foolishness of one's spirit corresponds directly to the unseen world. One must be extremely cautious.
:Although the above lists the outlines, the affairs of the unseen world are profound, spiritual, and wondrous, and there are aspects of their ultimate state that cannot be put into words. It depends solely on the practitioner's cultivation.
:The method of Returning to the Divine is most precious and most serious. For those who practice it, I will now show the essentials that must be known.
:'''Table of Methods for Returning to the Divine'''
:'''Formless (無形, ''Mukei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:'''Formed (有形, ''Yūkei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:* '''Total of Formed and Formless: 18 methods.'''
:* '''Realm of Demonic Phantoms (妖魅界, ''Yōmi-kai''):''' The same 18 methods as above. : '' The practices of shrine maidens (''miko'') or Hokke (Lotus Sutra) monks are of a lower-than-the-lowest-of-the-low grade, outside of these. : '' What is called the Realm of False Gods (''Jashin-kai'') is the same as the Realm of Demonic Phantoms. : '' These can be further divided into 362 methods.
:What is critical in Returning to the Divine is the Spirit Examiner (''Saniwa''). If one is not the right person for this role, it cannot be done. It requires one whose attention is thorough, who has courage, who possesses knowledge, and who is swift in clarifying principle and non-principle.
:1. One must inquire into the past, present, and future. :2. One must not proceed without discerning whether the spirit is a true god or a false god. :3. One must not proceed without knowing the upper, middle, and lower ranks of the gods. :4. One must not proceed without knowing the great deeds of the god. :5. One must not proceed without knowing the Wild Soul, Harmonious Soul, Fortunate Soul, and Wondrous Soul. :6. One must not be without the ability to distinguish between Heavenly Gods and Earthly Deities. :7. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have three grades. :8. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have public and private possessions/channels.
==Source== *[https://ja.wikisource.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E5%AD%A6%E6%8A%84 霊学抄] on Wikisource
[[Category:Honda Chikaatsu]]

revision.comment: /* English translation */
revision.text:
'''''Reigakushō''''' (霊学抄), or "A Summary of Spiritology," outlines the core tenets of the spiritual system developed by Honda Chikaatsu. It is a foundational document that explains his unique interpretation of ancient Shinto (''Ko-Shinto'') practices.
==Original text== ;霊学抄
皇国固有の霊学は鎮魂、帰神、太占の三にして、禁厭は鎮魂の作用の一なり。
=== 太占 === *古事記に伊邪那岐、伊邪那美に神が、「太占に卜へて」（布斗麻通にうらへて）とあるは無形を以て無形に対するもの、人間の思義す可らざるものなり。
*天岩戸の条に「天香山の真男鹿の肩を拔きて」とあるは、有形の太占なり。大甞祭のゆき、すきの御田を定むるは今日にても太占に依る。
=== 太占乃三大法 === *'''形象法'''。天地風雲其他一切萬の形を観じて判決す。 :鹿卜、亀卜、其他足占米占義占、法異なりと雖も皆之形象法の属とす。
*'''声音法'''。雷風其他万物の声音に聴て判決す。 :辻占、謡占、語声等一切声音法に属す。
*'''算数法'''。天数一より十に至り経緯の数理に依て判決す。故に是の如き法悉く皆算数法に属す。 :以上は現世の法にして神界にては無形に観じ無声に聴き無算に算へ給ふ。故に現世の法とは格別なるら知るべし。
=== 学則 === 神の黙示は則ち吾俯仰観察する宇宙の霊体力の三大を以てす。
*一、天地の真象を観察して真神の体を思考す可し。 *一、万有の運化の毫差無きを以て真神の力を思考す可し。 *一、活物の心性を覚悟して真神の霊魂を思考す可し。
以上の活経典有り真神の真神たる故由を知る。何ぞ人為の書巻を学習するを用ゐん哉。唯不変不易の真鑑実理ある而己。
'''体'''とは則ち物体にして化学の所謂元素之なり。
'''力'''とは則ち運動の力にして、地球の大陽を一周して春夏秋冬をなし、地球の自転して昼夜ら為す等皆力の処為なり。
'''霊'''とは則ち神也。吾人の霊魂亦之に属す。
=== 鎮魂 === 鎮魂の法は霊学の大本なれば、其の原由を論定し其の末法を講明せざる可らず。故に今皇典に拠て其由来を述ぶ。
伊邪那岐命曰く、天照大御神は高天原を知食すべしと詔玉ひて、御首玉を母由良に取り由良加して天照大御神に賜ひき。故に此玉を斎き奉りて御倉棚神と云ふ。是其霊魂を附着して現天の主宰たらしめん事を神定め賜ひしものなり。而して此玉ら天照大神より皇孫二二岐命へ御授けあり。其時の事実の古事記に見えて、男喜志玉男喜志鏡斂とあり。三種の神宝を帝位知食す御印として下し賜ひしょり以来、御代御代の帝王は申すも更なり、其御心を心として万民悉く尊奉崇敬して怠らざりし故に、神の神たる所以の理由よりして、万般の利益霊験ら蒙りし事国史に昭々として日月と共に光を争ふと云ふも我誣言に非るら知るべし。而して今本教に於るや更に一章を引証すべきなし。故に唯其招魂の作法を伝授せんとする也。
此鎮魂の法は天授の神法にして現世神界の学則なれば、上は天皇の治国平天下の御事よりして、下は人民修身斎家の基本、続て無形の神界を探知するの基礎なれば、宜敷朝夕之を懐中に秘し、事業の閑暇は謹て是を省み之を行ひ、霊魂の運転活動を学習すべし。尚詳細の事は後日講じ、引証を以て其基く処を現し、其霊妙なる処を感じ或は示して、益々天に代る大功力を千万世に建んとす。是則ち万物に長たるの義務なり。
:令義解に曰く
鎮者安也。人之陽気曰魂。言招離遊之運魂。留身体中府。
故謂之鎮魂。
=== 帰神 === 帰神の法を幽斎の法といふ。
神界に感合するの道は至尊至貴、濫に語る可き者に非ず。吾朝古典往々其実績を載すと雖も、中世祭祀の道衰へ其術を失ふ既に久し。神法に依り其古に復す。是即ち玄理の窮極、皇祖の以て皇孫に伝へし治国の大本にして祭祀の蘊奥なり。蓋し幽斎の法たる至厳至重、深く戒慎し其人に非れば行ふ可らざる者あり。濫に伝ふ可らざる意玆に存す。然りと雖も其精神萬難に撓まず。自ら彊めて止まされば竟に能く其妙境に達する事を得ん。後の此伝を受くる者厥れ之を諒せよ。幽冥に通ずるの道唯其れ專修に在り。玆に其法を示す。
*一、身体衣服を清潔にす可し。 *二、幽邃の地閑静の家を撰む可し。 *三、身体を整へ瞑目静座す可し。 *四、一切の妄想を除去す可し。 *五、感覚を蕩尽し意念を断滅す可し。 *六、心神を清澄にして感触の為に擾れざるを務む可し。 *七、一意に吾霊魂の天御中主大神の御許に至る事を黙念すべし。
右七章は自修の要を明らかにす。
幽斎は宇宙の主宰に感合し親しく八百万神に接す。其の修し得るに至ては至大無外、至小無内、無遠近、無大小、無広狭、無明暗、過去と現在と未来とを問はず一も通ぜざるは無し。是即ち惟神の妙法。
常に服膺すべき者あり。玆に其概を挙ぐ。
*一、霊魂は神界の賦与にして即ち分霊なれば、自ら之を尊重し妖魅等の為に誑かさる、事勿れ。 *二、正邪理非の分別を明かにす可し。 *三、常に神典を誦読し神徳を記憶す可し。 *四、幽冥に正神界と妖魅界と在る事を了得す可し。 *五、正神に百八十一の階級あり、妖魅亦之に同じ。 *六、正神界と邪神界とは正邪の別尊卑の差あり、其異る亦天淵の違あるを知る可し。 *七、精神正しければ即ち正神に感合し、邪なれば即ち邪神に感合す。精神の正邪賢愚は直に幽冥に応ず。最も戒慎すべし。
以上はその概を掲ぐと雖も、幽冥の事たる深遠霊妙、其至処は之を言ふ能はざる者あり。唯其人の修業に存す。
帰神の法たる至貴至重なり。行ふ者須く知る可きの要を玆に示す。
=== 帰神標目 === {| class="wikitable" |+ 無形 | 自感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 他感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 神感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |}
:合九法
{| class="wikitable" |+ 有形 | 自感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 他感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 神感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |}
:合九法
有形無形合拾八法。
妖魅界 前と同じく拾八法。
:巫或は法華僧の行は此等外下々下等也。
:邪神界と称するも妖魅界に同じ。
:之を分て三百六拾二法とす。
帰神に重要なるは審神者とす。其人に非れば即ち能はざる者也。其注意周到にして胆力あり、学識ありて理非を明にするに速かなるを要す。
*一、過去現在未来を伺ふ可し。 *二、真神なるや偽神なるや弁ぜずば在る可らず。 *三、神の上中下の品位を知らずば有る可らず。 *四、神の功業を知らずば有る可らず。 *五、荒魂和魂幸魂奇魂を知らずば有る可らず。 *六、天神地祇の分別無かる可らず。 *七、神に三等有るを知らずば有る可らず。 *八、神に公憑私憑あるを知らずば有る可らず。
==English translation== (Google Gemini 2.5 Pro translation; please check for errors and improvements)
:'''A Summary of Spiritology''' :''(Reigaku Shō)''
:The Spiritology (''Reigaku'') inherent to the Imperial Land consists of the three pillars of Soul Pacification (''Chinkon''), Returning to the Divine (''Kishin''), and Grand Divination (''Futomani''). Magical rites (''Kin'en'') are but one function of Soul Pacification.
:'''Grand Divination (太占, ''Futomani'')'''
:When the ''Kojiki'' states that the gods said to Izanagi and Izanami, "divine by Grand Divination" (''futomani ni uraete''), this refers to the formless confronting the formless—something beyond human thought or deliberation.
:The passage in the chapter on the Heavenly Rock Cave, which says, "the shoulder-bone of a true stag from Mount Ame-no-Kaguyama was extracted," refers to a formed type of Grand Divination. The selection of the fields for the ''Daijōsai'' (the first ceremonial offering of rice by a newly enthroned emperor) is, even today, determined by Grand Divination.
:'''The Three Great Methods of Grand Divination'''
:1. '''Method of Forms (形象法, ''Keishō-hō''):''' To make a judgment by observing the forms of heaven, earth, wind, clouds, and all other things. : '' Scapulimancy (divination by deer bones), plastromancy (divination by turtle shells), and other forms such as foot-reading, rice-reading, and righteousness-divination, though their methods differ, all belong to the Method of Forms.
:2. '''Method of Sounds (声音法, ''Seion-hō''):''' To make a judgment by listening to the sounds of thunder, wind, and all other things. : '' Crossroads-divination, song-divination, divination by the sound of a person's voice, and the like all belong to the Method of Sounds.
:3. '''Method of Numerology (算数法, ''Sansū-hō''):''' To make a judgment according to the mathematical principles of the celestial numbers from one to ten, arranged as warp and weft. : '' All methods of this kind belong to the Method of Numerology.
:The above are the methods of the manifest world. In the divine realm, the gods observe the formless, listen to the soundless, and calculate the numberless. Therefore, you should know that their methods are vastly different from the methods of the manifest world.
:'''Rules of Study (学則, ''Gakusoku'')'''
:The silent revelations of God are made through the three great principles of Spirit, Body, and Power, which we observe by looking up and down at the universe.
:1. One must, by observing the true forms of heaven and earth, contemplate the Body of the True God. :2. One must, by observing the flawless workings and transformations of all existence, contemplate the Power of the True God. :3. One must, by comprehending the heart and nature of living things, contemplate the Spirit-Soul of the True God.
:With this living scripture, one can know the reason why the True God is the True God. What use is there in studying man-made books and scrolls? There is only the unchanging and immutable true mirror and true principle.
:* '''Body (体, ''Tai'')''' refers to physical substance; it is what chemistry calls the elements. :* '''Power (力, ''Ryoku'')''' refers to the force of motion; the Earth's revolution around the great Sun creating the four seasons, and the Earth's own rotation creating day and night—these are all the workings of Power. :* '''Spirit (霊, ''Rei'')''' refers to the divine. Our own spiritual souls also belong to this.
:'''Soul Pacification (鎮魂, ''Chinkon'')'''
:The method of Soul Pacification is the great foundation of Spiritology, so it is imperative that we determine its origins and clarify its derivative methods. Therefore, I will now state its origins based on the Imperial Canons.
:The deity Izanagi-no-Mikoto declared, "Amaterasu-Ōmikami shall rule the High Plain of Heaven," and taking the string of beads from his divine neck and shaking them so they chimed, he bestowed them upon Amaterasu-Ōmikami. Therefore, this jewel is venerated and called the God of the August Storehouse Shelf (''Mikuratana-no-kami''). This was a divine decree meant to have her spirit-soul attach to it and make her the sovereign of the manifest heavens. And this jewel was then handed down from Amaterasu-Ōmikami to the Imperial Grandson, Ninigi-no-Mikoto. The facts of that event appear in the ''Kojiki'' as, "the beads of joy, the mirror of joy, keep them close." Since the Three Sacred Treasures were bestowed as the symbol of ruling the imperial throne, it goes without saying that the emperors of every generation—and all the myriad people who took the emperor's heart as their own—have revered and worshipped them without fail. For this reason, based on the very principle of why a god is a god, they have received all manner of benefits and spiritual efficacies. That this is recorded in our national histories, shining brightly and vying in radiance with the sun and moon, you should know is no falsehood of mine. As for our current teachings, there is not a single further passage to cite. Therefore, I will only transmit the procedure for this soul-summoning.
:This method of Soul Pacification is a divinely bestowed sacred law and a rule of study for both the manifest and divine worlds. It extends from the Emperor's work of governing the nation and pacifying the realm above, down to the people's foundation for cultivating the self and ordering the household below. It is furthermore the basis for perceiving the formless divine realm. Therefore, you should secretly carry this in your heart morning and evening, and in your leisure from work, you should reverently reflect upon it and practice it, learning the movements and activities of your spiritual soul. More detailed matters will be lectured on at a later date, where I will use citations to reveal its foundations and feel, or perhaps demonstrate, its spiritual wonders, so that we may increasingly establish, on behalf of heaven, a great and meritorious power for ten thousand generations. This is our duty as the head of all creation.
:The ''Ryō no Gige'' [an ancient commentary on law] states:
:鎮 (''Chin'') means to soothe (''an''). The yang energy of a person is called the soul (''kon''). This speaks of summoning the wandering vital soul (''un-kon'') and keeping it in the central office (''chū-fu'') of the body.
:Therefore, it is called Soul Pacification (''Chinkon'').
:'''Returning to the Divine (帰神, ''Kishin'')'''
:The method of Returning to the Divine is also called the method of Esoteric Rites (''Yūsai'').
:The way of achieving communion with the divine realm is most supreme and most precious; it is not something to be spoken of lightly. Although our nation's ancient classics frequently record its actual practice, the way of ritual decayed in the middle ages, and the art has now been lost for a long time. By relying on divine law, we can restore the ancient way. This is the ultimate of profound principles, the great foundation for ruling the nation that the Imperial Ancestors transmitted to the Imperial Grandson, and the esoteric heart of ritual. The method of Esoteric Rites is most strict and most serious; there are things that must not be practiced if one is not the right person. The intention not to transmit it carelessly lies here. Nevertheless, if your spirit does not bend before ten thousand difficulties and you strive for it yourself without stopping, you will eventually be able to reach its wondrous state. Let those who receive this transmission in the future understand this well. The way of communicating with the unseen world lies solely in dedicated practice. Here, I will show the method.
:1. One must cleanse one's body and clothes. :2. One must select a secluded place and a quiet house. :3. One must compose one's body, close one's eyes, and sit silently. :4. One must remove all delusive thoughts. :5. One must exhaust all sensory perception and extinguish all conceptual thought. :6. One must strive to keep one's heart-mind clear and pure, not to be disturbed by sensations. :7. One must, with single-minded focus, silently contemplate one's own spirit-soul reaching the presence of the great god Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Ōkami.
:The above seven articles clarify the essentials of self-cultivation.
:Through Esoteric Rites, one communes with the sovereign of the universe and comes into direct contact with the eight million gods. When one becomes capable of this practice, it is so great there is nothing outside it, and so small there is nothing inside it. There is no far or near, no large or small, no wide or narrow, no light or dark; from the past, to the present, to the future, there is nothing with which one cannot communicate. This is the wondrous method of acting as the gods do (''kannagara'').
:There are things that one should always cherish. Here I will list their outlines.
:1. The spirit-soul is a bestowal from the divine realm and is thus a divided spirit (''bunrei''). Therefore, respect it yourself and do not be deceived by phantoms, demons, and the like. :2. One must clearly distinguish between right and wrong, principle and non-principle. :3. One must always recite the divine canons and keep the virtues of the gods in memory. :4. One must fully comprehend that in the unseen world, there exists a realm of true gods and a realm of demonic phantoms. :5. The true gods have one hundred and eighty-one ranks; the demonic phantoms have the same. :6. One must know that the realm of true gods and the realm of false gods have a distinction between right and wrong and a difference in status, and that their variance is as great as that between heaven and the abyss. :7. If one's spirit is correct, one will commune with the true gods; if it is corrupt, one will commune with the false gods. The correctness, corruption, wisdom, or foolishness of one's spirit corresponds directly to the unseen world. One must be extremely cautious.
:Although the above lists the outlines, the affairs of the unseen world are profound, spiritual, and wondrous, and there are aspects of their ultimate state that cannot be put into words. It depends solely on the practitioner's cultivation.
:The method of Returning to the Divine is most precious and most serious. For those who practice it, I will now show the essentials that must be known.
:'''Table of Methods for Returning to the Divine'''
:'''Formless (無形, ''Mukei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:'''Formed (有形, ''Yūkei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:* '''Total of Formed and Formless: 18 methods.'''
:* '''Realm of Demonic Phantoms (妖魅界, ''Yōmi-kai''):''' The same 18 methods as above. : '' The practices of shrine maidens (''miko'') or Hokke (Lotus Sutra) monks are of a lower-than-the-lowest-of-the-low grade, outside of these. : '' What is called the Realm of False Gods (''Jashin-kai'') is the same as the Realm of Demonic Phantoms. : '' These can be further divided into 362 methods.
:What is critical in Returning to the Divine is the Spirit Examiner (''Saniwa''). If one is not the right person for this role, it cannot be done. It requires one whose attention is thorough, who has courage, who possesses knowledge, and who is swift in clarifying principle and non-principle.
:1. One must inquire into the past, present, and future. :2. One must not proceed without discerning whether the spirit is a true god or a false god. :3. One must not proceed without knowing the upper, middle, and lower ranks of the gods. :4. One must not proceed without knowing the great deeds of the god. :5. One must not proceed without knowing the Wild Soul, Harmonious Soul, Fortunate Soul, and Wondrous Soul. :6. One must not be without the ability to distinguish between Heavenly Gods and Earthly Deities. :7. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have three grades. :8. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have public and private possessions/channels.
==Source== *[https://ja.wikisource.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E5%AD%A6%E6%8A%84 霊学抄] on Wikisource
[[Category:Honda Chikaatsu]]

revision.comment: Applying the current category for this
revision.text:
'''''Reigakushō''''' (霊学抄), or "A Summary of Spiritology," outlines the core tenets of the spiritual system developed by Honda Chikaatsu. It is a foundational document that explains his unique interpretation of ancient Shinto (''Ko-Shinto'') practices.
==Original text== ;霊学抄
皇国固有の霊学は鎮魂、帰神、太占の三にして、禁厭は鎮魂の作用の一なり。
=== 太占 === *古事記に伊邪那岐、伊邪那美に神が、「太占に卜へて」（布斗麻通にうらへて）とあるは無形を以て無形に対するもの、人間の思義す可らざるものなり。
*天岩戸の条に「天香山の真男鹿の肩を拔きて」とあるは、有形の太占なり。大甞祭のゆき、すきの御田を定むるは今日にても太占に依る。
=== 太占乃三大法 === *'''形象法'''。天地風雲其他一切萬の形を観じて判決す。 :鹿卜、亀卜、其他足占米占義占、法異なりと雖も皆之形象法の属とす。
*'''声音法'''。雷風其他万物の声音に聴て判決す。 :辻占、謡占、語声等一切声音法に属す。
*'''算数法'''。天数一より十に至り経緯の数理に依て判決す。故に是の如き法悉く皆算数法に属す。 :以上は現世の法にして神界にては無形に観じ無声に聴き無算に算へ給ふ。故に現世の法とは格別なるら知るべし。
=== 学則 === 神の黙示は則ち吾俯仰観察する宇宙の霊体力の三大を以てす。
*一、天地の真象を観察して真神の体を思考す可し。 *一、万有の運化の毫差無きを以て真神の力を思考す可し。 *一、活物の心性を覚悟して真神の霊魂を思考す可し。
以上の活経典有り真神の真神たる故由を知る。何ぞ人為の書巻を学習するを用ゐん哉。唯不変不易の真鑑実理ある而己。
'''体'''とは則ち物体にして化学の所謂元素之なり。
'''力'''とは則ち運動の力にして、地球の大陽を一周して春夏秋冬をなし、地球の自転して昼夜ら為す等皆力の処為なり。
'''霊'''とは則ち神也。吾人の霊魂亦之に属す。
=== 鎮魂 === 鎮魂の法は霊学の大本なれば、其の原由を論定し其の末法を講明せざる可らず。故に今皇典に拠て其由来を述ぶ。
伊邪那岐命曰く、天照大御神は高天原を知食すべしと詔玉ひて、御首玉を母由良に取り由良加して天照大御神に賜ひき。故に此玉を斎き奉りて御倉棚神と云ふ。是其霊魂を附着して現天の主宰たらしめん事を神定め賜ひしものなり。而して此玉ら天照大神より皇孫二二岐命へ御授けあり。其時の事実の古事記に見えて、男喜志玉男喜志鏡斂とあり。三種の神宝を帝位知食す御印として下し賜ひしょり以来、御代御代の帝王は申すも更なり、其御心を心として万民悉く尊奉崇敬して怠らざりし故に、神の神たる所以の理由よりして、万般の利益霊験ら蒙りし事国史に昭々として日月と共に光を争ふと云ふも我誣言に非るら知るべし。而して今本教に於るや更に一章を引証すべきなし。故に唯其招魂の作法を伝授せんとする也。
此鎮魂の法は天授の神法にして現世神界の学則なれば、上は天皇の治国平天下の御事よりして、下は人民修身斎家の基本、続て無形の神界を探知するの基礎なれば、宜敷朝夕之を懐中に秘し、事業の閑暇は謹て是を省み之を行ひ、霊魂の運転活動を学習すべし。尚詳細の事は後日講じ、引証を以て其基く処を現し、其霊妙なる処を感じ或は示して、益々天に代る大功力を千万世に建んとす。是則ち万物に長たるの義務なり。
:令義解に曰く
鎮者安也。人之陽気曰魂。言招離遊之運魂。留身体中府。
故謂之鎮魂。
=== 帰神 === 帰神の法を幽斎の法といふ。
神界に感合するの道は至尊至貴、濫に語る可き者に非ず。吾朝古典往々其実績を載すと雖も、中世祭祀の道衰へ其術を失ふ既に久し。神法に依り其古に復す。是即ち玄理の窮極、皇祖の以て皇孫に伝へし治国の大本にして祭祀の蘊奥なり。蓋し幽斎の法たる至厳至重、深く戒慎し其人に非れば行ふ可らざる者あり。濫に伝ふ可らざる意玆に存す。然りと雖も其精神萬難に撓まず。自ら彊めて止まされば竟に能く其妙境に達する事を得ん。後の此伝を受くる者厥れ之を諒せよ。幽冥に通ずるの道唯其れ專修に在り。玆に其法を示す。
*一、身体衣服を清潔にす可し。 *二、幽邃の地閑静の家を撰む可し。 *三、身体を整へ瞑目静座す可し。 *四、一切の妄想を除去す可し。 *五、感覚を蕩尽し意念を断滅す可し。 *六、心神を清澄にして感触の為に擾れざるを務む可し。 *七、一意に吾霊魂の天御中主大神の御許に至る事を黙念すべし。
右七章は自修の要を明らかにす。
幽斎は宇宙の主宰に感合し親しく八百万神に接す。其の修し得るに至ては至大無外、至小無内、無遠近、無大小、無広狭、無明暗、過去と現在と未来とを問はず一も通ぜざるは無し。是即ち惟神の妙法。
常に服膺すべき者あり。玆に其概を挙ぐ。
*一、霊魂は神界の賦与にして即ち分霊なれば、自ら之を尊重し妖魅等の為に誑かさる、事勿れ。 *二、正邪理非の分別を明かにす可し。 *三、常に神典を誦読し神徳を記憶す可し。 *四、幽冥に正神界と妖魅界と在る事を了得す可し。 *五、正神に百八十一の階級あり、妖魅亦之に同じ。 *六、正神界と邪神界とは正邪の別尊卑の差あり、其異る亦天淵の違あるを知る可し。 *七、精神正しければ即ち正神に感合し、邪なれば即ち邪神に感合す。精神の正邪賢愚は直に幽冥に応ず。最も戒慎すべし。
以上はその概を掲ぐと雖も、幽冥の事たる深遠霊妙、其至処は之を言ふ能はざる者あり。唯其人の修業に存す。
帰神の法たる至貴至重なり。行ふ者須く知る可きの要を玆に示す。
=== 帰神標目 === {| class="wikitable" |+ 無形 | 自感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 他感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 神感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |}
:合九法
{| class="wikitable" |+ 有形 | 自感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 他感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 神感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |}
:合九法
有形無形合拾八法。
妖魅界 前と同じく拾八法。
:巫或は法華僧の行は此等外下々下等也。
:邪神界と称するも妖魅界に同じ。
:之を分て三百六拾二法とす。
帰神に重要なるは審神者とす。其人に非れば即ち能はざる者也。其注意周到にして胆力あり、学識ありて理非を明にするに速かなるを要す。
*一、過去現在未来を伺ふ可し。 *二、真神なるや偽神なるや弁ぜずば在る可らず。 *三、神の上中下の品位を知らずば有る可らず。 *四、神の功業を知らずば有る可らず。 *五、荒魂和魂幸魂奇魂を知らずば有る可らず。 *六、天神地祇の分別無かる可らず。 *七、神に三等有るを知らずば有る可らず。 *八、神に公憑私憑あるを知らずば有る可らず。
==English translation== (Google Gemini 2.5 Pro translation; please check for errors and improvements)
:'''A Summary of Spiritology''' :''(Reigaku Shō)''
:The Spiritology (''Reigaku'') inherent to the Imperial Land consists of the three pillars of Soul Pacification (''Chinkon''), Returning to the Divine (''Kishin''), and Grand Divination (''Futomani''). Magical rites (''Kin'en'') are but one function of Soul Pacification.
:'''Grand Divination (太占, ''Futomani'')'''
:When the ''Kojiki'' states that the gods said to Izanagi and Izanami, "divine by Grand Divination" (''futomani ni uraete''), this refers to the formless confronting the formless—something beyond human thought or deliberation.
:The passage in the chapter on the Heavenly Rock Cave, which says, "the shoulder-bone of a true stag from Mount Ame-no-Kaguyama was extracted," refers to a formed type of Grand Divination. The selection of the fields for the ''Daijōsai'' (the first ceremonial offering of rice by a newly enthroned emperor) is, even today, determined by Grand Divination.
:'''The Three Great Methods of Grand Divination'''
:1. '''Method of Forms (形象法, ''Keishō-hō''):''' To make a judgment by observing the forms of heaven, earth, wind, clouds, and all other things. : '' Scapulimancy (divination by deer bones), plastromancy (divination by turtle shells), and other forms such as foot-reading, rice-reading, and righteousness-divination, though their methods differ, all belong to the Method of Forms.
:2. '''Method of Sounds (声音法, ''Seion-hō''):''' To make a judgment by listening to the sounds of thunder, wind, and all other things. : '' Crossroads-divination, song-divination, divination by the sound of a person's voice, and the like all belong to the Method of Sounds.
:3. '''Method of Numerology (算数法, ''Sansū-hō''):''' To make a judgment according to the mathematical principles of the celestial numbers from one to ten, arranged as warp and weft. : '' All methods of this kind belong to the Method of Numerology.
:The above are the methods of the manifest world. In the divine realm, the gods observe the formless, listen to the soundless, and calculate the numberless. Therefore, you should know that their methods are vastly different from the methods of the manifest world.
:'''Rules of Study (学則, ''Gakusoku'')'''
:The silent revelations of God are made through the three great principles of Spirit, Body, and Power, which we observe by looking up and down at the universe.
:1. One must, by observing the true forms of heaven and earth, contemplate the Body of the True God. :2. One must, by observing the flawless workings and transformations of all existence, contemplate the Power of the True God. :3. One must, by comprehending the heart and nature of living things, contemplate the Spirit-Soul of the True God.
:With this living scripture, one can know the reason why the True God is the True God. What use is there in studying man-made books and scrolls? There is only the unchanging and immutable true mirror and true principle.
:* '''Body (体, ''Tai'')''' refers to physical substance; it is what chemistry calls the elements. :* '''Power (力, ''Ryoku'')''' refers to the force of motion; the Earth's revolution around the great Sun creating the four seasons, and the Earth's own rotation creating day and night—these are all the workings of Power. :* '''Spirit (霊, ''Rei'')''' refers to the divine. Our own spiritual souls also belong to this.
:'''Soul Pacification (鎮魂, ''Chinkon'')'''
:The method of Soul Pacification is the great foundation of Spiritology, so it is imperative that we determine its origins and clarify its derivative methods. Therefore, I will now state its origins based on the Imperial Canons.
:The deity Izanagi-no-Mikoto declared, "Amaterasu-Ōmikami shall rule the High Plain of Heaven," and taking the string of beads from his divine neck and shaking them so they chimed, he bestowed them upon Amaterasu-Ōmikami. Therefore, this jewel is venerated and called the God of the August Storehouse Shelf (''Mikuratana-no-kami''). This was a divine decree meant to have her spirit-soul attach to it and make her the sovereign of the manifest heavens. And this jewel was then handed down from Amaterasu-Ōmikami to the Imperial Grandson, Ninigi-no-Mikoto. The facts of that event appear in the ''Kojiki'' as, "the beads of joy, the mirror of joy, keep them close." Since the Three Sacred Treasures were bestowed as the symbol of ruling the imperial throne, it goes without saying that the emperors of every generation—and all the myriad people who took the emperor's heart as their own—have revered and worshipped them without fail. For this reason, based on the very principle of why a god is a god, they have received all manner of benefits and spiritual efficacies. That this is recorded in our national histories, shining brightly and vying in radiance with the sun and moon, you should know is no falsehood of mine. As for our current teachings, there is not a single further passage to cite. Therefore, I will only transmit the procedure for this soul-summoning.
:This method of Soul Pacification is a divinely bestowed sacred law and a rule of study for both the manifest and divine worlds. It extends from the Emperor's work of governing the nation and pacifying the realm above, down to the people's foundation for cultivating the self and ordering the household below. It is furthermore the basis for perceiving the formless divine realm. Therefore, you should secretly carry this in your heart morning and evening, and in your leisure from work, you should reverently reflect upon it and practice it, learning the movements and activities of your spiritual soul. More detailed matters will be lectured on at a later date, where I will use citations to reveal its foundations and feel, or perhaps demonstrate, its spiritual wonders, so that we may increasingly establish, on behalf of heaven, a great and meritorious power for ten thousand generations. This is our duty as the head of all creation.
:The ''Ryō no Gige'' [an ancient commentary on law] states:
:鎮 (''Chin'') means to soothe (''an''). The yang energy of a person is called the soul (''kon''). This speaks of summoning the wandering vital soul (''un-kon'') and keeping it in the central office (''chū-fu'') of the body.
:Therefore, it is called Soul Pacification (''Chinkon'').
:'''Returning to the Divine (帰神, ''Kishin'')'''
:The method of Returning to the Divine is also called the method of Esoteric Rites (''Yūsai'').
:The way of achieving communion with the divine realm is most supreme and most precious; it is not something to be spoken of lightly. Although our nation's ancient classics frequently record its actual practice, the way of ritual decayed in the middle ages, and the art has now been lost for a long time. By relying on divine law, we can restore the ancient way. This is the ultimate of profound principles, the great foundation for ruling the nation that the Imperial Ancestors transmitted to the Imperial Grandson, and the esoteric heart of ritual. The method of Esoteric Rites is most strict and most serious; there are things that must not be practiced if one is not the right person. The intention not to transmit it carelessly lies here. Nevertheless, if your spirit does not bend before ten thousand difficulties and you strive for it yourself without stopping, you will eventually be able to reach its wondrous state. Let those who receive this transmission in the future understand this well. The way of communicating with the unseen world lies solely in dedicated practice. Here, I will show the method.
:1. One must cleanse one's body and clothes. :2. One must select a secluded place and a quiet house. :3. One must compose one's body, close one's eyes, and sit silently. :4. One must remove all delusive thoughts. :5. One must exhaust all sensory perception and extinguish all conceptual thought. :6. One must strive to keep one's heart-mind clear and pure, not to be disturbed by sensations. :7. One must, with single-minded focus, silently contemplate one's own spirit-soul reaching the presence of the great god Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Ōkami.
:The above seven articles clarify the essentials of self-cultivation.
:Through Esoteric Rites, one communes with the sovereign of the universe and comes into direct contact with the eight million gods. When one becomes capable of this practice, it is so great there is nothing outside it, and so small there is nothing inside it. There is no far or near, no large or small, no wide or narrow, no light or dark; from the past, to the present, to the future, there is nothing with which one cannot communicate. This is the wondrous method of acting as the gods do (''kannagara'').
:There are things that one should always cherish. Here I will list their outlines.
:1. The spirit-soul is a bestowal from the divine realm and is thus a divided spirit (''bunrei''). Therefore, respect it yourself and do not be deceived by phantoms, demons, and the like. :2. One must clearly distinguish between right and wrong, principle and non-principle. :3. One must always recite the divine canons and keep the virtues of the gods in memory. :4. One must fully comprehend that in the unseen world, there exists a realm of true gods and a realm of demonic phantoms. :5. The true gods have one hundred and eighty-one ranks; the demonic phantoms have the same. :6. One must know that the realm of true gods and the realm of false gods have a distinction between right and wrong and a difference in status, and that their variance is as great as that between heaven and the abyss. :7. If one's spirit is correct, one will commune with the true gods; if it is corrupt, one will commune with the false gods. The correctness, corruption, wisdom, or foolishness of one's spirit corresponds directly to the unseen world. One must be extremely cautious.
:Although the above lists the outlines, the affairs of the unseen world are profound, spiritual, and wondrous, and there are aspects of their ultimate state that cannot be put into words. It depends solely on the practitioner's cultivation.
:The method of Returning to the Divine is most precious and most serious. For those who practice it, I will now show the essentials that must be known.
:'''Table of Methods for Returning to the Divine'''
:'''Formless (無形, ''Mukei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:'''Formed (有形, ''Yūkei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:* '''Total of Formed and Formless: 18 methods.'''
:* '''Realm of Demonic Phantoms (妖魅界, ''Yōmi-kai''):''' The same 18 methods as above. : '' The practices of shrine maidens (''miko'') or Hokke (Lotus Sutra) monks are of a lower-than-the-lowest-of-the-low grade, outside of these. : '' What is called the Realm of False Gods (''Jashin-kai'') is the same as the Realm of Demonic Phantoms. : '' These can be further divided into 362 methods.
:What is critical in Returning to the Divine is the Spirit Examiner (''Saniwa''). If one is not the right person for this role, it cannot be done. It requires one whose attention is thorough, who has courage, who possesses knowledge, and who is swift in clarifying principle and non-principle.
:1. One must inquire into the past, present, and future. :2. One must not proceed without discerning whether the spirit is a true god or a false god. :3. One must not proceed without knowing the upper, middle, and lower ranks of the gods. :4. One must not proceed without knowing the great deeds of the god. :5. One must not proceed without knowing the Wild Soul, Harmonious Soul, Fortunate Soul, and Wondrous Soul. :6. One must not be without the ability to distinguish between Heavenly Gods and Earthly Deities. :7. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have three grades. :8. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have public and private possessions/channels.
==Source== *[https://ja.wikisource.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E5%AD%A6%E6%8A%84 霊学抄] on Wikisource

revision.text:
'''''Reigakushō''''' (霊学抄), or "A Summary of Spiritology," outlines the core tenets of the spiritual system developed by Honda Chikaatsu. It is a foundational document that explains his unique interpretation of ancient Shinto (''Ko-Shinto'') practices.
==Original text== ;霊学抄
皇国固有の霊学は鎮魂、帰神、太占の三にして、禁厭は鎮魂の作用の一なり。
=== 太占 === *古事記に伊邪那岐、伊邪那美に神が、「太占に卜へて」（布斗麻通にうらへて）とあるは無形を以て無形に対するもの、人間の思義す可らざるものなり。
*天岩戸の条に「天香山の真男鹿の肩を拔きて」とあるは、有形の太占なり。大甞祭のゆき、すきの御田を定むるは今日にても太占に依る。

*'''声音法'''。雷風其他万物の声音に聴て判決す。 :辻占、謡占、語声等一切声音法に属す。
*'''算数法'''。天数一より十に至り経緯の数理に依て判決す。故に是の如き法悉く皆算数法に属す。 :以上は現世の法にして神界にては無形に観じ無声に聴き無算に算へ給ふ。故に現世の法とは格別なるら知るべし。
=== 学則 === 神の黙示は則ち吾俯仰観察する宇宙の霊体力の三大を以てす。
*一、天地の真象を観察して真神の体を思考す可し。 *一、万有の運化の毫差無きを以て真神の力を思考す可し。 *一、活物の心性を覚悟して真神の霊魂を思考す可し。
以上の活経典有り真神の真神たる故由を知る。何ぞ人為の書巻を学習するを用ゐん哉。唯不変不易の真鑑実理ある而己。
'''体'''とは則ち物体にして化学の所謂元素之なり。
'''力'''とは則ち運動の力にして、地球の大陽を一周して春夏秋冬をなし、地球の自転して昼夜ら為す等皆力の処為なり。
'''霊'''とは則ち神也。吾人の霊魂亦之に属す。
=== 鎮魂 === 鎮魂の法は霊学の大本なれば、其の原由を論定し其の末法を講明せざる可らず。故に今皇典に拠て其由来を述ぶ。
伊邪那岐命曰く、天照大御神は高天原を知食すべしと詔玉ひて、御首玉を母由良に取り由良加して天照大御神に賜ひき。故に此玉を斎き奉りて御倉棚神と云ふ。是其霊魂を附着して現天の主宰たらしめん事を神定め賜ひしものなり。而して此玉ら天照大神より皇孫二二岐命へ御授けあり。其時の事実の古事記に見えて、男喜志玉男喜志鏡斂とあり。三種の神宝を帝位知食す御印として下し賜ひしょり以来、御代御代の帝王は申すも更なり、其御心を心として万民悉く尊奉崇敬して怠らざりし故に、神の神たる所以の理由よりして、万般の利益霊験ら蒙りし事国史に昭々として日月と共に光を争ふと云ふも我誣言に非るら知るべし。而して今本教に於るや更に一章を引証すべきなし。故に唯其招魂の作法を伝授せんとする也。
此鎮魂の法は天授の神法にして現世神界の学則なれば、上は天皇の治国平天下の御事よりして、下は人民修身斎家の基本、続て無形の神界を探知するの基礎なれば、宜敷朝夕之を懐中に秘し、事業の閑暇は謹て是を省み之を行ひ、霊魂の運転活動を学習すべし。尚詳細の事は後日講じ、引証を以て其基く処を現し、其霊妙なる処を感じ或は示して、益々天に代る大功力を千万世に建んとす。是則ち万物に長たるの義務なり。
:令義解に曰く
鎮者安也。人之陽気曰魂。言招離遊之運魂。留身体中府。
故謂之鎮魂。
=== 帰神 === 帰神の法を幽斎の法といふ。
神界に感合するの道は至尊至貴、濫に語る可き者に非ず。吾朝古典往々其実績を載すと雖も、中世祭祀の道衰へ其術を失ふ既に久し。神法に依り其古に復す。是即ち玄理の窮極、皇祖の以て皇孫に伝へし治国の大本にして祭祀の蘊奥なり。蓋し幽斎の法たる至厳至重、深く戒慎し其人に非れば行ふ可らざる者あり。濫に伝ふ可らざる意玆に存す。然りと雖も其精神萬難に撓まず。自ら彊めて止まされば竟に能く其妙境に達する事を得ん。後の此伝を受くる者厥れ之を諒せよ。幽冥に通ずるの道唯其れ專修に在り。玆に其法を示す。
*一、身体衣服を清潔にす可し。 *二、幽邃の地閑静の家を撰む可し。 *三、身体を整へ瞑目静座す可し。 *四、一切の妄想を除去す可し。 *五、感覚を蕩尽し意念を断滅す可し。 *六、心神を清澄にして感触の為に擾れざるを務む可し。 *七、一意に吾霊魂の天御中主大神の御許に至る事を黙念すべし。
右七章は自修の要を明らかにす。
幽斎は宇宙の主宰に感合し親しく八百万神に接す。其の修し得るに至ては至大無外、至小無内、無遠近、無大小、無広狭、無明暗、過去と現在と未来とを問はず一も通ぜざるは無し。是即ち惟神の妙法。
常に服膺すべき者あり。玆に其概を挙ぐ。
*一、霊魂は神界の賦与にして即ち分霊なれば、自ら之を尊重し妖魅等の為に誑かさる、事勿れ。 *二、正邪理非の分別を明かにす可し。 *三、常に神典を誦読し神徳を記憶す可し。 *四、幽冥に正神界と妖魅界と在る事を了得す可し。 *五、正神に百八十一の階級あり、妖魅亦之に同じ。 *六、正神界と邪神界とは正邪の別尊卑の差あり、其異る亦天淵の違あるを知る可し。 *七、精神正しければ即ち正神に感合し、邪なれば即ち邪神に感合す。精神の正邪賢愚は直に幽冥に応ず。最も戒慎すべし。
以上はその概を掲ぐと雖も、幽冥の事たる深遠霊妙、其至処は之を言ふ能はざる者あり。唯其人の修業に存す。
帰神の法たる至貴至重なり。行ふ者須く知る可きの要を玆に示す。
=== 帰神標目 === {| class="wikitable" |+ 無形 | 自感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 他感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 神感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |}
:合九法
{| class="wikitable" |+ 有形 | 自感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 他感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |- | 神感法 || 上中下 || 三法 |}
:合九法
有形無形合拾八法。
妖魅界 前と同じく拾八法。
:巫或は法華僧の行は此等外下々下等也。
:邪神界と称するも妖魅界に同じ。
:之を分て三百六拾二法とす。
帰神に重要なるは審神者とす。其人に非れば即ち能はざる者也。其注意周到にして胆力あり、学識ありて理非を明にするに速かなるを要す。
*一、過去現在未来を伺ふ可し。 *二、真神なるや偽神なるや弁ぜずば在る可らず。 *三、神の上中下の品位を知らずば有る可らず。 *四、神の功業を知らずば有る可らず。 *五、荒魂和魂幸魂奇魂を知らずば有る可らず。 *六、天神地祇の分別無かる可らず。 *七、神に三等有るを知らずば有る可らず。 *八、神に公憑私憑あるを知らずば有る可らず。
==English translation== (Google Gemini 2.5 Pro translation; please check for errors and improvements)
:'''A Summary of Spiritology''' :''(Reigaku Shō)''
:The Spiritology (''Reigaku'') inherent to the Imperial Land consists of the three pillars of Soul Pacification (''Chinkon''), Returning to the Divine (''Kishin''), and Grand Divination (''Futomani''). Magical rites (''Kin'en'') are but one function of Soul Pacification.
:'''Grand Divination (太占, ''Futomani'')'''
:When the ''Kojiki'' states that the gods said to Izanagi and Izanami, "divine by Grand Divination" (''futomani ni uraete''), this refers to the formless confronting the formless—something beyond human thought or deliberation.
:The passage in the chapter on the Heavenly Rock Cave, which says, "the shoulder-bone of a true stag from Mount Ame-no-Kaguyama was extracted," refers to a formed type of Grand Divination. The selection of the fields for the ''Daijōsai'' (the first ceremonial offering of rice by a newly enthroned emperor) is, even today, determined by Grand Divination.
:'''The Three Great Methods of Grand Divination'''
:1. '''Method of Forms (形象法, ''Keishō-hō''):''' To make a judgment by observing the forms of heaven, earth, wind, clouds, and all other things. : '' Scapulimancy (divination by deer bones), plastromancy (divination by turtle shells), and other forms such as foot-reading, rice-reading, and righteousness-divination, though their methods differ, all belong to the Method of Forms.
:2. '''Method of Sounds (声音法, ''Seion-hō''):''' To make a judgment by listening to the sounds of thunder, wind, and all other things. : '' Crossroads-divination, song-divination, divination by the sound of a person's voice, and the like all belong to the Method of Sounds.
:3. '''Method of Numerology (算数法, ''Sansū-hō''):''' To make a judgment according to the mathematical principles of the celestial numbers from one to ten, arranged as warp and weft. : '' All methods of this kind belong to the Method of Numerology.
:The above are the methods of the manifest world. In the divine realm, the gods observe the formless, listen to the soundless, and calculate the numberless. Therefore, you should know that their methods are vastly different from the methods of the manifest world.
:'''Rules of Study (学則, ''Gakusoku'')'''
:The silent revelations of God are made through the three great principles of Spirit, Body, and Power, which we observe by looking up and down at the universe.
:1. One must, by observing the true forms of heaven and earth, contemplate the Body of the True God. :2. One must, by observing the flawless workings and transformations of all existence, contemplate the Power of the True God. :3. One must, by comprehending the heart and nature of living things, contemplate the Spirit-Soul of the True God.
:With this living scripture, one can know the reason why the True God is the True God. What use is there in studying man-made books and scrolls? There is only the unchanging and immutable true mirror and true principle.
:* '''Body (体, ''Tai'')''' refers to physical substance; it is what chemistry calls the elements. :* '''Power (力, ''Ryoku'')''' refers to the force of motion; the Earth's revolution around the great Sun creating the four seasons, and the Earth's own rotation creating day and night—these are all the workings of Power. :* '''Spirit (霊, ''Rei'')''' refers to the divine. Our own spiritual souls also belong to this.
:'''Soul Pacification (鎮魂, ''Chinkon'')'''
:The method of Soul Pacification is the great foundation of Spiritology, so it is imperative that we determine its origins and clarify its derivative methods. Therefore, I will now state its origins based on the Imperial Canons.
:The deity Izanagi-no-Mikoto declared, "Amaterasu-Ōmikami shall rule the High Plain of Heaven," and taking the string of beads from his divine neck and shaking them so they chimed, he bestowed them upon Amaterasu-Ōmikami. Therefore, this jewel is venerated and called the God of the August Storehouse Shelf (''Mikuratana-no-kami''). This was a divine decree meant to have her spirit-soul attach to it and make her the sovereign of the manifest heavens. And this jewel was then handed down from Amaterasu-Ōmikami to the Imperial Grandson, Ninigi-no-Mikoto. The facts of that event appear in the ''Kojiki'' as, "the beads of joy, the mirror of joy, keep them close." Since the Three Sacred Treasures were bestowed as the symbol of ruling the imperial throne, it goes without saying that the emperors of every generation—and all the myriad people who took the emperor's heart as their own—have revered and worshipped them without fail. For this reason, based on the very principle of why a god is a god, they have received all manner of benefits and spiritual efficacies. That this is recorded in our national histories, shining brightly and vying in radiance with the sun and moon, you should know is no falsehood of mine. As for our current teachings, there is not a single further passage to cite. Therefore, I will only transmit the procedure for this soul-summoning.
:This method of Soul Pacification is a divinely bestowed sacred law and a rule of study for both the manifest and divine worlds. It extends from the Emperor's work of governing the nation and pacifying the realm above, down to the people's foundation for cultivating the self and ordering the household below. It is furthermore the basis for perceiving the formless divine realm. Therefore, you should secretly carry this in your heart morning and evening, and in your leisure from work, you should reverently reflect upon it and practice it, learning the movements and activities of your spiritual soul. More detailed matters will be lectured on at a later date, where I will use citations to reveal its foundations and feel, or perhaps demonstrate, its spiritual wonders, so that we may increasingly establish, on behalf of heaven, a great and meritorious power for ten thousand generations. This is our duty as the head of all creation.
:The ''Ryō no Gige'' [an ancient commentary on law] states:
:鎮 (''Chin'') means to soothe (''an''). The yang energy of a person is called the soul (''kon''). This speaks of summoning the wandering vital soul (''un-kon'') and keeping it in the central office (''chū-fu'') of the body.
:Therefore, it is called Soul Pacification (''Chinkon'').
:'''Returning to the Divine (帰神, ''Kishin'')'''
:The method of Returning to the Divine is also called the method of Esoteric Rites (''Yūsai'').
:The way of achieving communion with the divine realm is most supreme and most precious; it is not something to be spoken of lightly. Although our nation's ancient classics frequently record its actual practice, the way of ritual decayed in the middle ages, and the art has now been lost for a long time. By relying on divine law, we can restore the ancient way. This is the ultimate of profound principles, the great foundation for ruling the nation that the Imperial Ancestors transmitted to the Imperial Grandson, and the esoteric heart of ritual. The method of Esoteric Rites is most strict and most serious; there are things that must not be practiced if one is not the right person. The intention not to transmit it carelessly lies here. Nevertheless, if your spirit does not bend before ten thousand difficulties and you strive for it yourself without stopping, you will eventually be able to reach its wondrous state. Let those who receive this transmission in the future understand this well. The way of communicating with the unseen world lies solely in dedicated practice. Here, I will show the method.
:1. One must cleanse one's body and clothes. :2. One must select a secluded place and a quiet house. :3. One must compose one's body, close one's eyes, and sit silently. :4. One must remove all delusive thoughts. :5. One must exhaust all sensory perception and extinguish all conceptual thought. :6. One must strive to keep one's heart-mind clear and pure, not to be disturbed by sensations. :7. One must, with single-minded focus, silently contemplate one's own spirit-soul reaching the presence of the great god Ame-no-Minakanushi-no-Ōkami.
:The above seven articles clarify the essentials of self-cultivation.
:Through Esoteric Rites, one communes with the sovereign of the universe and comes into direct contact with the eight million gods. When one becomes capable of this practice, it is so great there is nothing outside it, and so small there is nothing inside it. There is no far or near, no large or small, no wide or narrow, no light or dark; from the past, to the present, to the future, there is nothing with which one cannot communicate. This is the wondrous method of acting as the gods do (''kannagara'').
:There are things that one should always cherish. Here I will list their outlines.
:1. The spirit-soul is a bestowal from the divine realm and is thus a divided spirit (''bunrei''). Therefore, respect it yourself and do not be deceived by phantoms, demons, and the like. :2. One must clearly distinguish between right and wrong, principle and non-principle. :3. One must always recite the divine canons and keep the virtues of the gods in memory. :4. One must fully comprehend that in the unseen world, there exists a realm of true gods and a realm of demonic phantoms. :5. The true gods have one hundred and eighty-one ranks; the demonic phantoms have the same. :6. One must know that the realm of true gods and the realm of false gods have a distinction between right and wrong and a difference in status, and that their variance is as great as that between heaven and the abyss. :7. If one's spirit is correct, one will commune with the true gods; if it is corrupt, one will commune with the false gods. The correctness, corruption, wisdom, or foolishness of one's spirit corresponds directly to the unseen world. One must be extremely cautious.
:Although the above lists the outlines, the affairs of the unseen world are profound, spiritual, and wondrous, and there are aspects of their ultimate state that cannot be put into words. It depends solely on the practitioner's cultivation.
:The method of Returning to the Divine is most precious and most serious. For those who practice it, I will now show the essentials that must be known.
:'''Table of Methods for Returning to the Divine'''
:'''Formless (無形, ''Mukei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:'''Formed (有形, ''Yūkei'')''' :'' Self-Conduction Method (自感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' Other-Conduction Method (他感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) :'' God-Conduction Method (神感法) - Upper, Middle, Lower (3 methods) : * '''Total: 9 methods'''
:* '''Total of Formed and Formless: 18 methods.'''
:* '''Realm of Demonic Phantoms (妖魅界, ''Yōmi-kai''):''' The same 18 methods as above. : '' The practices of shrine maidens (''miko'') or Hokke (Lotus Sutra) monks are of a lower-than-the-lowest-of-the-low grade, outside of these. : '' What is called the Realm of False Gods (''Jashin-kai'') is the same as the Realm of Demonic Phantoms. : '' These can be further divided into 362 methods.
:What is critical in Returning to the Divine is the Spirit Examiner (''Saniwa''). If one is not the right person for this role, it cannot be done. It requires one whose attention is thorough, who has courage, who possesses knowledge, and who is swift in clarifying principle and non-principle.
:1. One must inquire into the past, present, and future. :2. One must not proceed without discerning whether the spirit is a true god or a false god. :3. One must not proceed without knowing the upper, middle, and lower ranks of the gods. :4. One must not proceed without knowing the great deeds of the god. :5. One must not proceed without knowing the Wild Soul, Harmonious Soul, Fortunate Soul, and Wondrous Soul. :6. One must not be without the ability to distinguish between Heavenly Gods and Earthly Deities. :7. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have three grades. :8. One must not proceed without knowing that gods have public and private possessions/channels.
==Source== *[https://ja.wikisource.org/wiki/%E9%9C%8A%E5%AD%A6%E6%8A%84 霊学抄] on Wikisource
[[Category:Honda Chikaatsu]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages November 4 2025]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

'''''Reigakushō''''' (霊学抄), or "A Summary of Spiritology," outlines the core tenets of the spiritual system developed by Honda Chikaatsu. It is a foundational document that explains his unique interpretation of ancient Shinto (''Ko-Shinto'') practices.
==Original text== ;霊学抄
皇国固有の霊学は鎮魂、帰神、太占の三にして、禁厭は鎮魂の作用の一なり。
=== 太占 === *古事記に伊邪那岐、伊邪那美に神が、「太占に卜へて」（布斗麻通にうらへて）とあるは無形を以て無形に対するもの、人間の思義す可らざるものなり。
*天岩戸の条に「天香山の真男鹿の肩を拔きて」とあるは、有形の太占なり。大甞祭のゆき、すきの御田を定むるは今日にても太占に依る。
=== 太占乃三大法 === *'''形象法'''。天地風雲其他一切萬の形を観じて判決す。 :鹿卜、亀卜、其他足占米占義占、法異なりと雖も皆之形象法の属とす。
*'''声音法'''。雷風其他万物の声音に聴て判決す。 :辻占、謡占、語声等一切声音法に属す。
*'''算数法'''。天数一より十に至り経緯の数理に依て判決す。故に是の如き法悉く皆算数法に属す。 :以上は現世の法にして神界にては無形に観じ無声に聴き無算に算へ給ふ。故に現世の法とは格別なるら知るべし。
=== 学則 === 神の黙示は則ち吾俯仰観察する宇宙の霊体力の三大を以てす。
*一、天地の真象を観察して真神の体を思考す可し。 *一、万有の運化の毫差無きを以て真神の力を思考す可し。 *一、活物の心性を覚悟して真神の霊魂を思考す可し。
以上の活経典有り真神の真神たる故由を知る。何ぞ人為の書巻を学習するを用ゐん哉。唯不変不易の真鑑実理ある而己。
'''体'''とは則ち物体にして化学の所謂元素之なり。
'''力'''とは則ち運動の力にして、地球の大陽を一周して春夏秋冬をなし、地球の自転して昼夜ら為す等皆力の処為なり。
'''霊'''とは則ち神也。吾人の霊魂亦之に属す。
=== 鎮魂 === 鎮魂の法は霊学の大本なれば、其の原由を論定し其の末法を講明せざる可らず。故に今皇典に拠て其由来を述ぶ。
伊邪那岐命曰く、天照大御神は高天原を知食すべしと詔玉ひて、御首玉を母由良に取り由良加して天照大御神に賜ひき。故に此玉を斎き奉りて御倉棚神と云ふ。是其霊魂を附着して現天の主宰たらしめん事を神定め賜ひしものなり。而して此玉ら天照大神より皇孫二二岐命へ御授けあり。其時の事実の古事記に見えて、男喜志玉男喜志鏡斂とあり。三種の神宝を帝位知食す御印として下し賜ひしょり以来、御代御代の帝王は申すも更なり、其御心を心として万民悉く尊奉崇敬して怠らざりし故に、神の神たる所以の理由よりして、万般の利益霊験ら蒙りし事国史に昭々として日月と共に光を争ふと云ふも我誣言に非るら知るべし。而して今本教に於るや更に一章を引証すべきなし。故に唯其招魂の作法を伝授せんとする也。
此鎮魂の法は天授の神法にして現世神界の学則なれば、上は天皇の治国平天下の御事よりして、下は人民修身斎家の基本、続て無形の神界を探知するの基礎なれば、宜敷朝夕之を懐中に秘し、事業の閑暇は謹て是を省み之を行ひ、霊魂の運転活動を学習すべし。尚詳細の事は後日講じ、引証を以て其基く処を現し、其霊妙なる処を感じ或は示して、益々天に代る大功力を千万世に建んとす。是則ち万物に長たるの義務なり。

鎮者安也。人之陽気曰魂。言招離遊之運魂。留身体中府。
故謂之鎮魂。
